text.title: Table-Driven Tests and Subtests
text.summary: Table-driven test pattern, t.Run subtests, t.Parallel(), loop-variable capture fix, t.Helper(), and test naming.

section.title: Table-Driven Pattern
section.rule: Use table-driven tests for any function with multiple input/output pairs — avoids copy-paste and makes cases explicit.
Define test cases as a slice of structs with named fields — never positional anonymous structs.

section.title: t.Parallel()
section.rule: Call t.Parallel() at the start of each subtest to run them concurrently.
When using t.Parallel() inside a range loop (Go < 1.22), capture the loop variable explicitly to avoid data races.
section.note: Go 1.22+ fixes loop variable semantics — the tc := tc capture is no longer needed but harmless.

section.title: assert vs require
section.rule: Use require (testify) when a failure should stop the test immediately — nil pointer dereferences, must-have preconditions.
Use assert (testify) for non-fatal checks where you want to see all failures.

section.title: t.Helper()
section.rule: Call t.Helper() in test helper functions so failure lines point to the caller, not the helper.
